capabilities.name: Evidence-led specialist handoff
capabilities.summary: Make every role return bounded scope, inspectable evidence, explicit blockers, residual risk, and a recommended next action.
method: State the exact scope handled and what was intentionally excluded.
Return structured evidence references instead of unsupported completion claims.
Separate findings, changes, blockers, skipped checks, and residual risk.
Give the Lead one concrete next action and an explicit status.
stopConditions: Required evidence cannot be produced or safely referenced.
The requested scope or permission boundary is ambiguous.
A blocker would make a success claim misleading.
loopControls: Each iteration must record new evidence or a changed hypothesis.
Unresolved blockers prevent finalization.

capabilities.name: Minimal coherent change
capabilities.summary: Constrain implementation to the smallest coherent surface that satisfies the request and expose follow-ups instead of smuggling them into the diff.
method: Read the task literally and identify the minimum affected surface.
Justify each changed file against an acceptance criterion.
Walk the final diff for unrelated cleanup, speculative abstraction, and hidden behavior changes.
List useful but out-of-scope follow-ups without implementing them.
stopConditions: The requested behavior cannot be isolated without changing scope.
An adjacent refactor is required but was not authorized.
The diff contains unrelated cleanup or speculative flexibility.
loopControls: Reject iterations that add unrelated files or abstractions.
Reconfirm acceptance coverage after every scope expansion.

capabilities.name: Codebase orientation and execution tracing
capabilities.summary: Build a source-grounded repository map and trace real entry-to-output paths before proposing changes.
method: Inventory runtime, entry points, configuration, tests, and top-level boundaries.
Trace the relevant path from input through orchestration to side effects and output.
Cite inspected files and distinguish source facts from hypotheses.
Mark unknowns and high-risk boundaries before edits begin.
stopConditions: The authoritative entry point or runtime cannot be identified.
A proposed conclusion is not grounded in inspected source.
Generated or vendored code is being mistaken for authoritative source.
loopControls: Record a falsifiable hypothesis before modifying a traced path.
A repeated failure must produce a revised execution trace.

capabilities.name: Developer tooling and command contracts
capabilities.summary: Treat CLI, MCP, installer, packaging, errors, and machine-readable output as stable developer-facing contracts.
method: Define human-readable and machine-readable behavior together.
Make errors name the failing field, boundary, and safe corrective action.
Preserve compatibility for command names, schemas, exit behavior, and packaging.
Test fresh installation, version parity, startup, and failure paths.
stopConditions: A command or schema change silently breaks existing callers.
Generated and canonical artifacts disagree.
An error path leaks a stack trace, secret, or raw model content by default.
loopControls: Run parity validation whenever canonical artifacts change.
Treat a fresh-install failure as a completion blocker.

capabilities.name: Multi-agent systems engineering
capabilities.summary: Apply hierarchical orchestration, least privilege, schema-bound handoffs, explicit recovery, contradiction handling, and traceable execution to agent workflows.
method: Use a Lead-owned hierarchical topology unless another topology is explicitly justified.
Bind each specialist to a role, capability, permission boundary, input contract, and output schema.
Enumerate timeout, partial result, contradiction, cascade, and stale-context failure paths.
Emit correlated metadata without storing raw prompts, tool arguments, or outputs.
stopConditions: A specialist can expand its own permissions or delegate further.
Required context would be silently truncated.
Contradictory specialist findings are unresolved.
Raw prompts, tool arguments, secrets, or unredacted outputs would enter telemetry.
loopControls: Bound retries and stop repeated identical failure signatures.
Validate each specialist result before synthesis.
Require explicit reconciliation of contradictions.

capabilities.name: Workflow and state architecture
capabilities.summary: Map actors, triggers, state transitions, side effects, branches, failure recovery, and ownership across user and system workflows.
method: Identify actors, triggers, preconditions, transitions, side effects, and terminal states.
Map happy paths, alternate branches, retries, timeouts, cancellation, and recovery.
Tie each workflow claim to routes, workers, migrations, config, or orchestration source.
Assign ownership and acceptance evidence to every material transition.
stopConditions: A state transition lacks an owner or observable outcome.
Retries or cancellation could duplicate side effects.
A destructive terminal path lacks explicit approval and recovery.
loopControls: Bind each phase to a named state transition and acceptance evidence.
Do not advance while recovery behavior for a failed transition is unknown.

capabilities.name: API and protocol compatibility
capabilities.summary: Review API and tool contracts for schema clarity, compatibility, versioning, deprecation, errors, limits, and integrator experience.
method: Treat the published schema and observable behavior as the contract.
Classify every change as additive, behavior-changing, or breaking.
Define stable error shape, bounds, idempotency, and deprecation behavior.
Test old callers, malformed input, unknown fields, limits, and partial failures.
stopConditions: A breaking change lacks explicit versioning and migration treatment.
Unknown or malformed input fails open.
Error behavior is ambiguous or leaks sensitive implementation detail.
loopControls: Re-run compatibility evidence after every contract change.
A breaking contract change requires a revised goal and approval.

capabilities.name: Database reliability and safe change
capabilities.summary: Review data changes for durability, migration safety, expand-contract compatibility, access enforcement, recovery, and operational proof.
method: Identify data invariants, access boundaries, transaction behavior, and rollback limits.
Prefer backward-compatible expand-contract migrations for live systems.
Prove backup usability with restore evidence when recovery claims matter.
Check locking, long-running backfills, replication, connection, and partial-failure risk.
stopConditions: A destructive migration lacks backup, rollback, and explicit approval.
Access control is asserted only in application code when database enforcement is required.
A restore or rollback claim has not been tested at the required risk level.
loopControls: A migration phase must preserve old and new readers until contract completion.
Data mutation criteria require explicit verifier evidence.

capabilities.name: Incident and service reliability
capabilities.summary: Apply observable incident command, mitigation-first response, rollback readiness, service objectives, and blameless causal learning.
method: Establish impact, timeline, owner, communications, and an observable mitigation target.
Prefer reversible mitigation before speculative root-cause changes.
Define service signals, rollback triggers, monitoring windows, and escalation paths.
Separate contributing system conditions from blame and capture durable follow-up.
stopConditions: Production mutation lacks explicit authority, owner, rollback, or monitoring.
Impact cannot be measured or the mitigation cannot be observed.
The proposed action increases blast radius without a bounded reason.
loopControls: Circuit-break on repeated operational failure signatures.
Require a rollback trigger and monitoring window before release-like progression.

capabilities.name: Identity and access control
capabilities.summary: Review authentication, authorization, sessions, service identities, least privilege, secrets, and auditable access decisions across trust boundaries.
method: Map principals, credentials, trust boundaries, token lifetime, and authorization decision points.
Enforce least privilege at the authoritative boundary, not only in presentation code.
Check provisioning, revocation, session invalidation, elevation, and audit events.
Treat exposed secrets as compromised and require rotation evidence.
stopConditions: Authorization relies on user-editable metadata or client-only checks.
A privileged credential crosses into a public or lower-trust boundary.
Revocation or privilege-elevation behavior is undefined.
loopControls: Security-sensitive criteria require negative authorization evidence.
Credential exposure stops the loop until revocation and rotation are addressed.

capabilities.name: Accessibility assurance
capabilities.summary: Combine standards-based automation with manual keyboard, focus, semantics, contrast, dynamic-content, and assistive-technology checks.
method: Define the applicable standard and affected user flows.
Use automation for breadth and manual interaction for semantics, focus, keyboard, and announcements.
Record component, expected behavior, observed behavior, severity, and remediation.
Distinguish checks performed from checks unavailable in the current environment.
stopConditions: A release-critical flow cannot be completed by keyboard.
Critical state or error information is unavailable to assistive technology.
Automation is being presented as complete accessibility proof.
loopControls: User-interface completion requires both automated and applicable manual evidence.
Unavailable assistive-technology checks must remain explicit residual risk.

capabilities.name: Performance engineering
capabilities.summary: Establish reproducible baselines, representative workloads, percentiles, resource signals, budgets, and statistically honest before/after evidence.
method: Define workload, environment, warm-up, sample size, metrics, and acceptance budget before measuring.
Capture baseline and candidate results under comparable conditions.
Report distributions and resource behavior, not only a single average.
Tie optimization to a measured bottleneck and re-run correctness checks.
stopConditions: Baseline and candidate environments are not comparable.
A performance claim lacks workload, sample, or percentile evidence.
An optimization changes correctness or exceeds an agreed resource budget.
loopControls: Performance iterations must cite a measured bottleneck and comparable baseline.
Stop optimization when the agreed budget is met or evidence quality degrades.

capabilities.name: AI-assisted code security
capabilities.summary: Audit AI-assisted and model-enabled code for leaked secrets, authorization gaps, unsafe trust boundaries, prompt injection, excessive tool authority, and false assurance.
method: Trace secrets, privileged clients, identities, untrusted content, model instructions, and tool authority end to end.
Show exact evidence and exploit path before assigning a security finding.
Keep external content as data and schema-validate model or specialist output before use.
State scan scope, blind spots, confidence, and rotation steps for exposed secrets.
stopConditions: Untrusted content can become higher-priority instructions or privileged tool arguments.
A service credential or secret can reach a client bundle, log, trace, or model context.
A security pass claim exceeds the checks actually performed.
loopControls: Model-enabled changes require adversarial negative tests at trust boundaries.
Do not finalize while privileged tool authority is broader than the verified need.

capabilities.name: Technical compliance assurance
capabilities.summary: Map scoped controls to current state, evidence, gaps, target state, remediation, ownership, and residual risk without substituting checklists for technical proof.
method: Define the applicable framework, systems, period, control objective, and evidence owner.
Map each finding to current state, target state, concrete evidence, remediation, and residual risk.
Test whether the technical control operates, not merely whether documentation exists.
Separate observed evidence from management assertion and unavailable evidence.
stopConditions: A compliance conclusion depends only on an assertion or policy document.
The audit scope, period, or control objective is undefined.
Required evidence contains secrets, personal data, or regulated data that cannot be safely minimized.
loopControls: Each control criterion must name its verifier and evidence owner.
Unavailable operating evidence remains an explicit blocker or qualification.
